summary: Full backend + frontend test of CareerOS multi-source job aggregator. Found and FIXED a critical caching bug (timezone-naive vs offset-aware datetime in _cached_scrape causing 500s on every cached search). All 14 pytest backend tests pass. Frontend E2E: landing, demo login, browse (75 cards from RemoteOK+YC+HN), search filter, save->saved, profile, analytics (2 charts), and the AI modal (RUN MATCH + DRAFT LETTER + Copy) all work end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED in server.py _cached_scrape: previous code compared cached['expires_at'] (naive UTC from Mongo) with datetime.now(timezone.utc) (aware) which raised TypeError, causing every cached /api/jobs/search to return 500. Fixed by normalizing tz to UTC before compare.
Per-source 'status' field in /api/jobs/search response is a plain string per source (e.g. {'remoteok': 'ok'}) — consider richer object {status, count, error} for easier client diagnostics.
auth/register accepts any password >=6 chars — no complexity / breach check (acceptable for MVP, flagging).
No rate limiting on AI endpoints — Claude calls are expensive (5-15s). Consider per-user throttle.
Frontend localStorage JWT — XSS would leak token. Acceptable per spec (JWT primary, Google later), worth noting.
test_credentials: demo@careeros.io / demo1234 (pre-seeded with profile). admin@careeros.io / admin1234.
seed_data_creation: Test created 1 transient registered user (TEST_*@example.com) and saved/deleted 1 saved-job. CRUD tests cleaned up after themselves.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py with shared fixtures for demo_token + auth_headers + shared search payload. AI tests use a hard-coded job dict so they don't depend on scraper output. Frontend modal trigger is the 'Details + AI' button on each job card (clicking the card itself does NOT open the modal — this appears intentional). All sources (RemoteOK, YC, HN) returning live jobs; Wellfound is empty as expected (DataDome bot blocked).
rca of the issue: Cache TTL bug RCA: motor/pymongo returns BSON datetime as naive (no tzinfo) on read; comparison with timezone-aware datetime.now(timezone.utc) raised TypeError, surfacing as 500 Internal Server Error on every subsequent /api/jobs/search request hitting the cache. Reproduction: hit /api/jobs/search twice within 10 min. Mitigation: in _cached_scrape, attach timezone.utc to the read-back expires_at before comparing (applied).
